strategy: Kill order is the fight. If Skeletaur dies before or in the same round as his minions, he revives to 100% via a Moon Blessing, so his Death Lemurs must die first, then Skeletaur on a later round.

Do not rely on Feint. He converts any Feint on him to half value (70% becomes 35%) and Indemnity does not stop it, so use the Balance version of Feint, which does not trigger the cheat, or lean on blades.

Bring stun blocks and expect confusion. His natural attack is followed by a 2-round Confuse and a 2-round Shadow Stun, and he Beguiles a random wizard at the start of Rounds 4 and 5, so protect your hitter.
Manage the totems. Killing his last minion makes him summon an Armor, Hex, or Taunting Totem, so plan to clear minions in a way that still leaves you a clean window on the boss afterward.
He is Shadow with 68% Balance resist and no boost school, so avoid Balance and stack blades on any other school; join on time since late wizards eat his attack plus a Stun.
Clear the Lemurs first, deal with the summoned totem, then stack blades (Balance Feint if used) and burst Skeletaur down a round after the minions are gone so the revive cannot trigger.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard is hit by Skeletaur's natural attack, which also triggers his Shadow Stun on that wizard; be present at the start.
If Skeletaur dies before or in the same round as his minions, he casts a Moon Blessing and revives to 100%. Kill the minions first, then him a round later.
Feint on him is halved (70% becomes a 35% Trap), so its value is cut.
Indemnity does not protect your Feint from the halving cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger the halving, so use it if you want full-value trap pressure.
After his natural attack he casts a 2-round Confuse on the target wizard.
After his natural attack he also casts a 2-round Shadow Stun, so carry stun blocks for your hitter.
At the start of Rounds 4 and 5 he Beguiles a random wizard, so watch for forced attacks on your own team.
Killing his last remaining minion makes him summon an Armor, Hex, or Taunting Totem in its place.
If he has multiple minions defeated and ends with none, he casts the summon spell repeatedly but no extra minion actually spawns.
